gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The two supported serialization formats (spec §3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Both formats share the same self-describing grammar (`[Repr] ‖ [Meta] ‖"] # [doc = " [payload]`), so the round-trip property holds for each independently.  The"] # [doc = " only difference is the byte representation: `Wire` is JSON-in-bytes (the"] # [doc = " same substrate used for the `Value` serde form in M-104), `Json` is the"] # [doc = " UTF-8 text form suitable for human/tool consumption (G11 dual projection)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN-able selection"] # [doc = " A `Format` value is the reified, inspectable selection artifact (C3): the"] # [doc = " choice of `Wire` vs `Json` is visible at every call site — there is no"] # [doc = " ambient default that silently changes the wire form (spec §7-Q5 / RFC-0016"] # [doc = " §8-Q3 tension A; required-explicit until the per-ring ergonomics pass M-540)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum Format { # [doc = " The self-describing `[Repr] ‖ [Meta] ‖ [payload]` binary-JSON form"] # [doc = " (RFC-0001 §4.8).  The byte representation is a compact JSON object"] # [doc = " encoded as UTF-8 bytes (not a distinct binary encoding — see FLAG §8-Q6"] # [doc = " for a future binary wire form; the grammar is identical to `Json` but"] # [doc = " the container is `Vec<u8>`)."] Wire , # [doc = " The **one canonical JSON projection** — compact UTF-8 JSON text."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `fmt.to_json` (M-533) **delegates** to this format (README §5 seam):"] # [doc = " one projection, two entry points.  The round-trip property is"] # [doc = " established here and shared — not duplicated.  (Delegation is FLAGGED"] # [doc = " §7-Q1 pending maintainer sign-off.)"] Json , }
gaps.reason: enum `Format` derive(Clone) is a satisfied no-op under Mycelium's value semantics (ADR-003 — every value already copies structurally; DN-128 §6.1) — not emitted as an impl, not a gap

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Project `v` to the wire/JSON byte form for the given `format`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (when `Ok`)"] # [doc = " A faithful projection: every JSON-representable `Value` has a wire/JSON form (RFC-0001 §4.8)."] # [doc = " `serialize` borrows `v` immutably; it never mutates or re-keys the value, so the content hash"] # [doc = " is unchanged (C4/ADR-003)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility: `Err(SerError::OutOfDomain)`"] # [doc = " A `Value` carrying a non-finite `f64` (`NaN`/`±∞`) is refused — JSON cannot represent it and"] # [doc = " `serde_json` would silently emit `null` (a lossy, identity-colliding encoding). Never-silent"] # [doc = " (C1/G2). Every other well-formed `Value` serializes (M-104's `serde` impl is total over the"] # [doc = " finite domain)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [doc = " Pure computation over the in-memory value; no IO."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN-able: n/a"] # [doc = " A faithful projection has no hidden selection or approximation (spec §4/C3)."] pub fn serialize (v : & Value , format : Format) -> Result < Vec < u8 > , SerError > { check_json_representable (v) ? ; let bytes = match format { Format :: Wire | Format :: Json => { serde_json :: to_vec (v) . expect ("Value serialization is total over finite Values (M-104)") } } ; Ok (bytes) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Recover a `Value` from `bytes` serialized in the given `format`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Empirical` (round-trip property; spec §4.2)"] # [doc = " `deserialize(serialize(v, f), f) ≡ v` holds over a generated property-test"] # [doc = " corpus (asserted in `#[cfg(test)]`).  The tag is **`Empirical`** — not"] # [doc = " `Proven` — because no injectivity/totality theorem over the closed grammar"] # [doc = " has been checked here (VR-5 / spec §7-Q2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility: `Err(SerError)` with a locus (C1 — never-silent)"] # [doc = " Any of the five failure modes below; the error carries the **byte offset or"] # [doc = " field path** of the failure (RFC-0013 I1):"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - `Truncated{at}` — input ended before a complete value was decoded."] # [doc = " - `Malformed{at, why}` — bytes do not parse (grammar violation)."] # [doc = " - `UnknownTag{path, tag}` — unrecognized `Repr`/ctor/`Meta` tag."] # [doc = " - `OutOfDomain{path, why}` — field decodes but violates a value-model"] # [doc = "   invariant (e.g. payload length ≠ repr width)."] # [doc = " - `BudgetExceeded{kind}` — a declared decode budget overrun (ADR-015)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **No partially-filled `Value` is ever returned** (C1/G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [doc = " Pure over the byte input; no IO."] pub fn deserialize (bytes : & [u8] , _format : Format) -> Result < Value , SerError > { serde_json :: from_slice :: < Value > (bytes) . map_err (| e | map_serde_error (e , bytes)) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Map a `serde_json::Error` (from `from_slice`) to a typed [`SerError`] with"] # [doc = " the best available locus information."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `serde_json::Error` exposes `line()` and `column()` (1-based); we convert to"] # [doc = " a byte-offset approximation using the line/column and the raw input (best-effort"] # [doc = " — a precise byte offset would require a byte-counting deserializer, which is"] # [doc = " deferred to a future codec improvement; this is the honest `Empirical` floor)."] fn map_serde_error (e : serde_json :: Error , input : & [u8]) -> SerError { let byte_offset = ByteOffset (approx_byte_offset (input , e . line () , e . column ())) ; let msg = e . to_string () ; if is_truncated_error_msg (& msg , input) { SerError :: Truncated { at : byte_offset } } else if is_unknown_tag_error (& msg) { SerError :: UnknownTag { path : FieldPath :: from_static ("repr") , tag : extract_unknown_tag (& msg) , } } else if is_domain_error (& msg) { SerError :: OutOfDomain { path : classify_path_from_message (& msg) , why : msg , } } else { SerError :: Malformed { at : byte_offset , why : msg , } } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Compute an approximate byte offset from a 1-based `(line, column)` pair and"] # [doc = " the raw input bytes.  Returns `input.len()` if the line/column is out of range"] # [doc = " (e.g. for truncated inputs where serde_json reports line 1/col 1 on empty)."] fn approx_byte_offset (input : & [u8] , line : usize , col : usize) -> u64 { if input . is_empty () || line == 0 { return input . len () as u64 ; } let mut current_line = 1usize ; let mut line_start = 0usize ; for (i , & b) in input . iter () . enumerate () { if current_line == line { let col_offset = col . saturating_sub (1) ; return (line_start + col_offset) . min (input . len ()) as u64 ; } if b == b'\n' { current_line += 1 ; line_start = i + 1 ; } } input . len () as u64 }
